query: I have a Claude Code skill at /Users/me/repo/.claude/skills/pdf-extractor with about 600 lines in SKILL.md. The description starts with 'I extract PDFs' and there's a README.md inside the folder. Audit it against Anthropic's authoring rules, fix what's safe, and report what was applied versus what was deferred for manual review.

Polish the harness skill at .claude/skills/harness — preserve its purpose verbatim but identify and fix any frontmatter violations, layout issues, or style problems. Apply low-risk fixes automatically, defer high-risk ones, and produce a final categorized report (critical/structural/stylistic/opportunity).

I'm reviewing my skill at skills/data-extractor before shipping. The frontmatter description is 1100 characters and contains XML-style placeholders. Audit against Anthropic's canonical rules, identify each violation by section + severity + location, and apply non-regressing fixes.

Got a SKILL.md at /tmp/my-skill that doesn't trigger when I expect it to. Run a full audit of the skill, focusing on description quality and progressive disclosure. Apply improvements that preserve the stated purpose. Skip anything risky and tell me what was skipped.

Refactor the SKILL.md at skills/migrations-runner. The body is 700 lines, references are nested two levels deep, and the description doesn't mention the file types it works with. Bring it into compliance with Anthropic's authoring rules without changing the skill's purpose.

Polish the email-parser skill at .claude/skills/email-parser. Audit the frontmatter, layout, body style, and description quality. Apply None/Low/Medium-risk fixes autonomously per the regression-risk grading. Defer High-risk findings to a final report.

Audit the docs-writer skill at skills/docs-writer/ and apply any safe improvements automatically. The skill description currently uses first-person voice and the SKILL.md body is 800 lines. Preserve the stated purpose; report what's deferred.

I have a brand new skill at .claude/skills/legal-analyzer that I want to ship. Run the canonical audit checklist against it, categorize findings by severity, and produce a final report. Apply autonomous fixes for anything below High regression risk.

Audit the skill at skills/chart-builder for conformance with the SKILL.md format rules. The frontmatter has angle brackets in the description and the folder contains a README.md. Apply mechanical fixes to bring it into compliance, preserving its stated purpose.

Run a full audit on the report-generator skill at .claude/skills/report-generator and apply any fixes that won't change what the skill does. Specifically check whether the description includes negative triggers and whether the body uses third-person imperative throughout.

Create a brand new Claude Code skill from scratch for processing CSV files with custom delimiter handling. Generate proper YAML frontmatter, write the SKILL.md body with progressive disclosure, and include a scripts/ directory with a parse-csv.py utility.

I want to evaluate whether my pdf-extractor skill produces correct outputs on real test prompts. Run it against 5 sample PDFs and grade the extraction quality. Compare with-skill versus without-skill outputs and produce a benchmark.
Package my skill at skills/data-extractor as a .skill file so I can distribute it. Generate the package, validate it, and tell me where the output landed.
What are Anthropic's best practices for writing Claude Code skills? Walk me through the frontmatter rules, directory layout, and progressive disclosure model with concrete examples.
Benchmark my skill's runtime performance — measure pass rate, token usage, and time vs the baseline (without the skill loaded). Run 5 iterations and aggregate the results.
Review this pull request for code quality issues. The diff touches /src/auth/login.ts and /src/auth/login.test.ts. Look for type safety, error handling, and test coverage gaps.
Audit my Python project at /Users/me/projects/api for security vulnerabilities, especially around the authentication middleware and input validation in /src/handlers/.
Improve the description in my README.md at /Users/me/repo/README.md so users understand what the project does and how to get started.
Check my MCP server's tool descriptions for clarity and completeness. The server exposes 8 tools and I want to make sure each description is unambiguous and follows MCP best practices.
Create test cases for skill-improver — write 10 eval prompts that exercise edge cases like skills with multi-level nesting, very long bodies, and mixed-language content.
